contest.title: 2024-2025 ICPC Asia Jakarta Regional Contest (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Read the full statement and constraints: |S|,|T|<=200000; find a shortest abbreviation admitting at least two nonempty prefix/suffix splits.

Read the official Jakarta analysis. It represents two splits as nonempty P,Q,R, proves Q can be shortened to one character without changing the abbreviation, and minimizes over 26 characters in linear time.

Opened and read the published solutions. They independently use the one-character overlap reduction, the earliest valid occurrence in S, and the latest valid occurrence in T to construct a shortest answer.
Exhaustively compared the one-character-overlap construction with all abbreviations for every binary S,T of lengths up to 5, checking both optimal length and two-split validity.

annotation.summary: Construct the shortest string that can be split in at least two distinct positions into a prefix of S followed by a suffix of T, or report impossibility.
annotation.constraints: 1<=|S|,|T|<=200000 over lowercase English letters; a linear scan with constant-size character tables is required.
annotation.core_idea: Two distinct split positions give a representation Z=P+Q+R with P,Q,R all nonempty, P+Q a prefix of S, and Q+R a suffix of T. If |Q|>1, remove the last character of Q and prepend it to R; Z is unchanged and both prefix/suffix conditions remain true. Repeating yields an equally short witness with |Q|=1. For overlap character c, choose an index i in S with 1<=i<|S| and S[i]=c, and j in T with 0<=j<|T|-1 and T[j]=c. The candidate is S[0:i]+T[j:|T|], of length i+|T|-j. For each c, the earliest valid i and latest valid j minimize this independently; choose the best of the 26 candidates.
annotation.complexity: O(|S|+|T|+26) time and O(26) auxiliary memory, excluding the output string.
tricks: Turn two split points into three nonempty pieces P,Q,R sharing the middle overlap.
Shift characters from Q into R until the shared overlap has length one without changing the abbreviation.
For each overlap letter, combine its earliest eligible occurrence in S with its latest eligible occurrence in T.

solution_variants.name: single-character-overlap-greedy
solution_variants.summary: Reduce every feasible two-split abbreviation to a one-character overlap, store the earliest eligible S occurrence and latest eligible T occurrence for each letter, and output the minimum-length combination.
solution_variants.complexity: O(|S|+|T|) time and O(26) auxiliary memory.

tags.evidence: The decisive model is the three-part decomposition Z=P+Q+R induced by two valid split positions, with P+Q constrained as a prefix and Q+R as a suffix.
After reducing Q to one character c, choosing the earliest eligible c in S and latest eligible c in T independently minimizes i+|T|-j for that c; taking the best character is globally optimal.
For each fixed overlap letter, the optimum pushes its S occurrence as far left and its T occurrence as far right as eligibility permits.
